title: sporobolus indicus subsp. purpureosuffusus
intro: Sporobolus indicus subsp. purpureosuffusus, also known as Purple False Mannagrass, is a type of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Sporobolus indicus subsp. purpureosuffusus include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used as a paste or powder for topical applications.
